stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing on the Soque River is currently fair to good with a quality score of 6.0. Recent stocking boosts confidence in using egg patterns, squirmy worms, and attractor nymphs.
generated_text.best_window: The best window is the morning of day 1 due to lower winds and stable sky cover, providing calmer conditions ideal for clean drifts and effective presentations.
generated_text.current_conditions: Current flow is 64 cfs with a gage height of 3.55 ft. Water temperature and turbidity data are unavailable.
generated_text.weather_setup: Skies are partly cloudy with about 20% average cover. Pressure and its trend are unavailable. Winds are light from the ESE at 2.2 mph with gusts up to 11.5 mph. Rain probability is minimal with expected precipitation near zero.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will favor comfortable feeding lanes and stay near cover as light levels rise. Use smaller natural patterns and stocked trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracell nymphs. Prioritize clean drifts and be prepared to adjust as trout may not be fully aggressive.
